news.headline: Global NFT market to reach USD 84.13 billion by 2029, growing at 30.3% CAGR due to rising demand for digital art.
news.summary: A recent report analyzing the impact of AI on market trends reveals that the global market size for non-fungible tokens (NFTs) is projected to increase by USD 84.13 billion between 2025 and 2029. This growth is expected to occur at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 30.3% throughout the forecast period.

news.headline: Global emergency food market to increase by $3.20bn from 2024-2028 at CAGR of 7.35%, driven by organized retail sector growth.
news.summary: A recent report utilizing AI technology indicates significant growth in the global emergency food market. According to Technavio, the market is projected to increase by USD 3.20 billion between 2024 and 2028. The forecast suggests a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 7.35% during this period. This growth trend highlights the increasing demand for emergency food products on a global scale.

news.headline: Former Malaysian PM and Jupiter DEX victim of hack promoting fake coins to public.
news.summary: During a recent string of hacks, the accounts of decentralized exchange aggregator Jupiter and a previous Malaysian prime minister were compromised to endorse fraudulent coins. The cyber attackers utilized this opportunity to promote scam coins through the hijacked accounts. This incident raised concerns about the security measures in place for high-profile accounts, as it highlighted vulnerabilities in the systems. The exact number of compromised accounts and the extent of the impact are still being investigated by authorities. Such breaches underscore the importance of enhanced cybersecurity protocols to prevent unauthorized access to accounts and prevent the promotion of illicit activities.

news.headline: Israeli Army Radio: Israeli defense minister instructs army to prepare a plan to allow Gaza residents to leave the strip

New Wave Group AB's year-end report 2024: Net sales increased by 3% to SEK 2,827.3 million in Q4, with operating result at SEK 462.7 million. For the full year, net sales reached SEK 9,528.7 million, with an operating result of SEK 1,262.3 million. Proposed dividend of SEK 3.50 per share and CEO notes strong growth in the USA and Central Europe.
news.summary: A company in Gothenburg reported financial results for October-December 2024 and the full year. In Q4, net sales rose by 3% to SEK 2,827.3 million, with operating result at SEK 462.7 million and earnings per share at SEK 2.60. For the full year, net sales were steady at SEK 9,528.7 million, with operating result at SEK 1,262.3 million and earnings per share at SEK 6.63. Cash flow from operating activities also varied. The equity ratio was 63.7%, while the net debt to equity ratio was 25.2%. The CEO mentioned challenges in the market, particularly in Sweden, with sales growth being weaker than desired. The company proposed a dividend of SEK 3.50 per share at the upcoming general meeting.

news.headline: ECB's Cipollone: No recession is seen, a soft landing is still the main scenario
ECB's Cipollone: US tariffs on China could force Beijing to dump goods on Europe, weighing on growth and inflation.
ECB's Cipollone: There is no evidence of possible inflation undershoot, risks are balanced.
Thailand resumes central bank chair selection process after extended delay.
ECB's Cipollone: there is still room for adjusting rates downward, inflation is almost on target
ECB's Cipollone: Economic fundamentals are not significantly different than in December projections; rate cuts are coherent with declining inflation
Qualcomm posts record $10.1 billion in Q1 revenue, but lower Q2 guidance due to seasonality and Apple shipments. Stock falls 4.6%.
news.summary: Despite Qualcomm Inc. (NASDAQ: QCOM) beating earnings expectations and posting a record first-quarter revenue of $10.1 billion, its shares dropped by 4.6% in after-hours trading due to weak second-quarter guidance. The company's QCT segment showed significant growth of 20% year-over-year, but CFO and COO Akash Palkhiwala indicated expectations of $8.9 billion to $9.5 billion in QCT revenues for the upcoming quarter. This decline was attributed to seasonality, reduced shipments to Apple, and a lower number of flagship launches impacting earnings-per-share. The stock price fell to $167.77 per share despite the strong performance in the first quarter.

news.headline: Bitcoin thoughts of Czech central bank governor raise concerns, adds pressure amid criticism.
Korea Zinc safeguards critical metals supply amidst trade tensions, ensuring stability in global supply chain. Feb 4 China export controls announced.
news.summary: On February 6, 2025, in Seoul, South Korea, amidst escalating U.S.-China trade tensions leading to export controls on rare metals, Korea Zinc (KRX:010130) has emerged as a crucial player in stabilizing the supply chain for essential minerals. China's announcement on February 4 highlights the growing strategic significance of Korea Zinc in ensuring the availability of critical minerals amid the current global economic uncertainties.

news.headline: Massive line highlights severity of job crisis in South Africa, with thousands waiting in queue.
China retaliates with tariffs on US goods following 10% tariff imposed by Trump, also launching an investigation into Google.
news.summary: Following the enforcement of a 10% tariff on Chinese goods on Tuesday, China responded with retaliatory actions, imposing tariffs on imports and initiating an investigation into Google.

news.headline: UK economy facing challenges as Reeves battles against the current situation.
Ginseng market set to grow by $195.03 million (2024-2028) due to health benefits awareness, powered by AI trends.
news.summary: In a recent report on the impact of AI on market trends, it was revealed that the global ginseng market is projected to increase by approximately USD 195.03 million between 2024 and 2028, as stated by Technavio. This growth is expected to occur at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 5.05% throughout the forecast period.

news.headline: Custom Apparel Market to grow by $2.45B by 2029; boosted by internet and smartphone usage. AI impacting market.
news.summary: In a recent report focusing on the impact of AI on market dynamics, it has been revealed that the global custom apparel market is projected to experience significant growth, with an estimated increase of USD 2.45 billion between 2025 and 2029. The study conducted by Technavio forecasts a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 8.4% for the market during this period, highlighting the role of artificial intelligence in reshaping the industry landscape.

news.headline: ArcelorMittal plans $1.2 billion investment in Alabama
ArcelorMittal moves forward with Alabama steel plant plans
BenevolentAI plans to delist from Euronext Amsterdam via merger with Osaka Holdings, shifting to private structure. Shareholder approval needed.
news.summary: In a recent announcement, BenevolentAI, a leader in AI-powered pharmaceutical innovation, disclosed plans for a strategic move. The company, listed on Euronext Amsterdam (Ticker: BAI), aims to delist pending shareholder agreement. This decision involves merging BenevolentAI with Osaka Holdings S.à r.l., facilitating the shift to a private entity. The merger will lead to a streamlining of the company’s share configuration.

news.headline: OM SYSTEM launches OM-3 Mirrorless Camera, blending heritage style with advanced features for photographers on the go.
news.summary: Combining traditional heritage design elements with cutting-edge technology, OM Digital Solutions unveiled their most recent advancements in digital imaging with the introduction of the OM SYSTEM OM-3 Mirrorless Interchangeable Lens Camera. The camera is set to revolutionize the industry with its innovative features and sleek design. This announcement was made in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania on February 6, 2025.

news.headline: CTP signs 64,000 sqm leases with Serbian logistics company Milšped, expanding their operations to 105,000 sqm.
news.summary: In Amsterdam, CTP, the largest listed developer, owner, and manager of industrial and logistics properties in Europe based on gross lettable area (GLA), recently inked three new leases with Milšped. Milšped, a major logistics provider for Serbia and Southeast Europe, will lease a total of 64,000 sqm from CTP. This deal boosts Milšped’s total space with CTP to 105,000 sqm, showcasing a substantial growth in its operations and catering to the rising need for logistics services across the region.

news.headline: Telenor Group reports solid Q4 results, increased cash flow, proposes dividend of NOK 9.60 per share for 2024, focusing on growth in 2025.
news.summary: During the fourth quarter, Telenor Group reported robust performance, surpassing projected cash flow expectations. The company is staying on course with its strategic initiatives, focusing on growth and transformation while cutting down on capital expenditure for 2025. The Board of Directors have suggested a dividend of NOK 9.60 per share for the year 2024.

news.headline: NORBIT will reveal Q4 2024 results on Feb 13, 2025, in Trondheim, Norway. Global provider of technology to specific applications.
news.summary: In Trondheim, Norway on February 6th, 2025, NORBIT, a leading provider of specialized technology for specific applications, is set to release its fourth-quarter earnings for 2024 on Thursday, February 13th, 2025.

news.headline: ArcelorMittal reports Q4 2024 and full year 2024 results. Leading steel company announces performance data.
news.summary: Luxembourg, on February 6, 2025, ArcelorMittal, a global leader in steel and mining, disclosed its financial results for the quarter and year concluding on December 31, 2024. The company is listed as MT on stock exchanges in New York, Amsterdam, Paris, and Luxembourg, and as MTS in Madrid.

news.headline: 2024 Annual Report: Hearing and Gaming up, Enterprise down with 1% revenue growth, 12% EBITA margin, DKK 1.1B free cash flow (excl. M&A).
Engelhart and e.optimum sign German wind PPA for 86MW capacity until Dec 2025, enough for 35,000 households.
news.summary: In Berlin, Engelhart, the Global Commodities and Renewable Energy Trader, has signed a new Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with e.optimum, a major independent energy supplier in Germany. This agreement ensures the purchase of renewable energy from specific German onshore wind assets, with the electricity to be delivered from February to December 2025. The deal involves a total capacity of around 86MW, which will generate sufficient power to supply approximately 35,000 households.

news.headline: IBA signs contract with AIG for Proteus®ONE system in Hyderabad, India.  Proton therapy system to be at AIG Hospital.
news.summary: In Louvain-La-Neuve, Belgium on February 06, 2025, IBA (Ion Beam Applications S.A., EURONEXT) – the global leader in particle accelerator technology and primary supplier of proton therapy solutions for cancer treatment – has confirmed a signed contract with the Asian Institute of Gastroenterology (AIG). The agreement entails the provision of a Proteus®ONE1 compact proton therapy system, to be situated at the upcoming Oncology Centre in AIG Hospitals’ Gachibowli campus, located in Hyderabad, India. The initial payment for the system has already been received.

news.headline: Nordecon Group's 2024 saw improved profitability and revenue at €223,925K, with a gross profit margin of 7.5% (9% in Q4). €5,162K net profit in 2024. Order book €209,489K as of Dec 31, 2024. 12 months saw new contracts worth €181,437K.
news.summary: In 2024, Nordecon Group saw a successful year with improved profitability and increased revenue. The general contracting market remains competitive with low developer confidence, and no significant growth in investments or the construction market is expected for 2025. The group's gross profit margin for 2024 was 7.5%, a significant increase from the previous year. Net profit amounted to €5,162 thousand compared to a loss in 2023. Revenue for 2024 was €223,925 thousand, a 20% increase from the previous year, with a 36% increase in the Buildings segment and a 25% decrease in the Infrastructure segment. Key projects included Saku Upper Secondary School and Loodusmaja (Nature Hub). The order book as of December 31, 2024, was €209,489 thousand, providing a stable base for 2025. New contracts worth €181,437 thousand were signed over the year, with €49,636 thousand signed in the fourth quarter.

news.headline: DNO ASA approves cash dividend of NOK 0.3125 per share, payable on 21 Feb 2025 to shareholders of record on 14 Feb 2025. Ex-dividend trading starts on 13 Feb 2025.
news.summary: In Oslo on February 6, 2025, DNO ASA, a Norwegian oil and gas operator, revealed that following authorization obtained at the Annual General Meeting on June 6, 2024, the Board of Directors has given the green light for a dividend payout of NOK 0.3125 per share. This dividend is scheduled to be disbursed around February 21, 2025, to shareholders listed as of February 14, 2025. Trading of DNO shares excluding this dividend is set to commence on February 13, 2025.

news.headline: DNO ASA reports 2024 revenues of $667 million with strong production in Kurdistan and North Sea growth.
news.summary: On February 6, 2025, DNO ASA, a Norwegian oil and gas company, announced a total revenue of $667 million for the year 2024. This strong financial performance was driven by robust production activities in the Kurdistan region of Iraq, amidst ongoing expansion efforts in the North Sea.

news.headline: Kraken co-founder Jesse Powell sues, alleging housing denial in San Francisco due to crypto connections.
news.summary: Jesse Powell, one of the co-founders of Kraken, has recently filed a housing discrimination lawsuit against a San Francisco building management. The lawsuit claims that the management prevented a sale involving Powell due to his association with cryptocurrency. The case highlights issues of discrimination in housing arrangements and raises concerns about prejudices against individuals involved in the crypto sector.

ECB's Cipollone suggests potential for rate cuts, as inflation nears target
Cipollone from ECB: Soft landing still main scenario, no recession anticipated

Airties and Qualcomm team up to demonstrate platform for 5G Fixed Wireless Operators at Mobile World Congress to improve network performance.
news.summary: The Airties Cloud platform now offers monitoring of 5G Fixed Wireless Access (FWA) link performance, providing a comprehensive overview of Quality of Experience (QoE) for Wide Area Network (WAN) and Local Area Network (LAN) networks. This enhanced visibility is achieved through the use of Qualcomm® 5G Fixed Wireless Access Platforms. FWA operators can now have insights into connection issues and proactively address them either automatically or through actionable steps. This feature allows operators to ensure a seamless and high-quality connectivity experience for users.

news.headline: Facility Management Market in UK to grow by USD 19.6 billion (2024-2028) with CAGR of 4.6%, AI redefining landscape.
news.summary: In New York on February 6, 2025, an AI-driven market report revealed that the facility management market in the UK is projected to witness significant growth, with an estimated increase of USD 19.6 billion expected from 2024 to 2028. The market is forecasted to grow at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 4.6% during this period.

news.headline: Global AR and VR market to reach USD 442.99 billion by 2028, with 50.22% CAGR growth fueled by AI.
news.summary: A recent report analyzing the impact of artificial intelligence on market trends revealed promising projections for the global augmented reality and virtual reality market. According to Technavio, the market is expected to experience significant growth, with an estimated increase of USD 442.99 billion from 2024 to 2028. The market is anticipated to expand at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 50.22% during this period. The potential for substantial growth in this sector underscores the increasing prominence of emerging technologies and their influence on consumer behavior and market dynamics.

Nearly three-quarters of institutional traders, 71%, have "no intentions" to invest in cryptocurrency, per JPMorgan survey.
news.summary: Health officials in the city reported a total of 387 new cases of COVID-19, bringing the total number of confirmed cases to 10,562. Unfortunately, 14 more deaths were also recorded, bringing the total number of fatalities to 286. These numbers highlight the ongoing severity of the pandemic and the importance of following safety guidelines to prevent further spread of the virus. In response to the increasing cases, authorities have urged the public to adhere to social distancing measures and wear masks in public places. The healthcare system is under strain due to the rising number of cases, reinforcing the need for collective action to combat the virus.

news.headline: Swedish firm introduces Avalanche, Cardano ETP on Nasdaq Helsinki, offering Finland investors new opportunities.
news.summary: A new offering by Swedish crypto asset manager Virtune is allowing investors in Finland to access Avalanche and Cardano through recently introduced crypto ETP products.

news.headline: Global reinsurance market to grow by USD 539.3 billion (2025-2029) at 12.2% CAGR, driven by AI impact on market trends.
news.summary: A recent report highlights the impact of AI on the market, indicating that the global reinsurance market is projected to increase significantly by USD 539.3 billion between 2025 and 2029, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 12.2% during this period. This growth signifies a shifting landscape in the market, driven by technological advancements and evolving consumer demands.

news.headline: Semiconductor market to grow by $157.1bn by 2029, driven by IoT adoption. AI reshaping landscape.
news.summary: In a recent report utilizing AI technology, it has been projected that the global semiconductor market is set to increase by a substantial USD 157.1 billion within the period of 2025 to 2029. The market is expected to experience a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 4.5% throughout this forecasted period. This growth signifies significant opportunities for stakeholders in the market.

news.headline: Global personalized gifts market to grow by USD 10.76 billion by 2029, driven by introduction of new products & AI impact.
news.summary: In a recent report, it was highlighted that AI is reshaping the market scene. The global market for personalized gifts is projected to witness a substantial increase of USD 10.76 billion between 2025 and 2029, as outlined by Technavio. During this period, the market is expected to experience a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 6.7%, showcasing significant development prospects.

news.headline: Tamura says gap in monetary policy view with market is narrowing
